name: Starlette
what_it_does: Lightweight ASGI web framework for Python — the foundation that FastAPI is built on, providing routing, middleware, WebSocket, background tasks, and static files without FastAPI's pydantic overhead. Starlette features: Router with routes(), Mount for sub-apps, Request/Response objects, WebSocket support, StaticFiles, BackgroundTask, Middleware (CORS, GZip, HTTPS redirect, TrustedHost, Sessions), TestClient for sync testing, HTTPException, StreamingResponse, FileResponse, JSONResponse, HTMLResponse, RedirectResponse, and BaseHTTPMiddleware for custom middleware. Ideal for lightweight ASGI apps where FastAPI's validation overhead is unnecessary.
use_cases: Agent lightweight API — from starlette.applications import Starlette; from starlette.routing import Route; from starlette.responses import JSONResponse; async def agent_endpoint(request): data = await request.json(); return JSONResponse({'status': 'ok', 'result': process(data)}); app = Starlette(routes=[Route('/agent', agent_endpoint, methods=['POST'])]) — minimal ASGI app; agent tool server without FastAPI overhead; manual request/response handling

Agent middleware stack — from starlette.middleware import Middleware; from starlette.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware; from starlette.middleware.gzip import GZipMiddleware; app = Starlette(middleware=[Middleware(CORSMiddleware, allow_origins=['*']), Middleware(GZipMiddleware)]) — compose middleware; agent API with CORS + compression; add custom middleware with BaseHTTPMiddleware

Agent WebSocket endpoint — from starlette.websockets import WebSocket; async def ws_endpoint(websocket: WebSocket): await websocket.accept(); while True: data = await websocket.receive_text(); await websocket.send_text(process(data)); app = Starlette(routes=[WebSocketRoute('/ws', ws_endpoint)]) — native WebSocket in Starlette; agent real-time communication without FastAPI

Agent background tasks — from starlette.background import BackgroundTask; async def endpoint(request): task = BackgroundTask(send_notification, email='user@example.com'); return JSONResponse({'ok': True}, background=task) — background task after response sent; agent webhook handler returns 200 immediately then processes; task runs after response delivered to client

Agent static files + routing — from starlette.staticfiles import StaticFiles; from starlette.routing import Mount, Route; app = Starlette(routes=[Route('/api/v1', api_handler), Mount('/static', StaticFiles(directory='static'), name='static')]) — mixed API + static files; agent dashboard serves static frontend + API backend from single ASGI app; Mount for sub-application composition
not_for: Schema validation — Starlette has no built-in pydantic integration; for request/response validation use FastAPI which builds on Starlette
Automatic API documentation — Starlette has no OpenAPI/Swagger generation; use FastAPI for automatic docs
Teams unfamiliar with ASGI — Starlette requires understanding of ASGI protocol; FastAPI with pydantic is more approachable for beginners
best_when: Lightweight ASGI applications where FastAPI's pydantic validation overhead is unnecessary — building agent tool servers, proxies, WebSocket handlers, and microservices with manual request parsing.
avoid_when: You need automatic request validation (use FastAPI), OpenAPI docs (use FastAPI), or are building consumer-facing APIs where pydantic's type safety is valuable.

auth.notes: No built-in auth. SessionMiddleware for cookie sessions. Bearer token via custom middleware or dependency. AuthenticationMiddleware with custom authenticator.

pricing.notes: Starlette is BSD licensed. Free for all use.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits. ~10-20% faster than FastAPI for same routes (no pydantic overhead). Comparable to raw ASGI performance. Uvicorn + Starlette: ~50K req/sec for simple JSON endpoints on modern hardware.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: HTTPException with status_code and detail for HTTP errors. WebSocketDisconnect when client disconnects. RequestValidationError not raised (no built-in validation). StreamingResponse for chunked responses. Error handling via exception handlers registered on app. Error messages are clear HTTP-level errors.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Starlette routing is deterministic. Request handling is as idempotent as handler functions. BackgroundTask execution is not idempotent if task has side effects. WebSocket connections are stateful.
known_agent_gotchas: request.json() and request.body() can only be called once — Starlette streams request body; await request.json() consumes the stream; calling it again raises RuntimeError or returns empty; agent middleware that reads body must: body = await request.body(); request._body = body to cache for subsequent reads; or pass body through request state

No pydantic validation by default — Starlette accepts any request body; agent code must manually validate: data = await request.json(); if 'required_field' not in data: return JSONResponse({'error': 'missing field'}, status_code=422); or add FastAPI on top; missing validation leads to unhandled KeyError/TypeError in handlers

WebSocket must be explicitly accepted — async def handler(ws): # must call accept() before receiving; await ws.accept(); await ws.receive_text() — forgetting accept() causes WebSocket handshake timeout; client sees connection fail; always accept() before any receive/send operations

Mount() path matching is prefix not exact — Mount('/api', sub_app) matches /api, /api/v1, /api/anything; Route('/api', handler) matches only exact /api; agent apps mixing Mount and Route must order specific routes before Mount to prevent Mount swallowing specific paths

TestClient is synchronous wrapper — from starlette.testclient import TestClient; client = TestClient(app); response = client.get('/') — TestClient is sync even for async handlers; runs event loop internally; agent tests using TestClient cannot use pytest-asyncio; use httpx.AsyncClient(app=app) for async testing

exception_handlers must return Response — @app.exception_handler(HTTPException); async def http_exception_handler(request, exc): return JSONResponse({'detail': exc.detail}, status_code=exc.status_code) — handler must return Response object; returning None causes 'NoneType is not a callable' error; agent error handlers must always return valid Response

security_score_components.security_notes: ASGI framework with no built-in auth or input validation. Validate all input manually or use FastAPI. TrustedHostMiddleware prevents host header injection. HTTPSRedirectMiddleware forces HTTPS. CORS configuration: restrict allow_origins to specific domains in production. Session secret key must be cryptographically random and kept secret.
